title: Carrying the Torch: 815th Airlift Squadron Assumes Airlift Mission in Africa
description: Airmen assigned to the Minnesota Air National Guard 133rd Airlift Wing concluded a historic deployment to Africa, as airmen assigned to the Air Force Reserve 815th Airlift Squadron, known as the Flying Jennies, are carrying the torch for the next rotation.

body.text: There was no formal ceremony marking the transition at Camp Lemonnier, Djibouti. No guidon passed from one commander to another. Instead, a moment represented something larger; the passing of a torch that lights the path of Air Force airlift across the U.S. Africa Command area of responsibility.

An Air Force C-130H3 Hercules taxis in the U.S. Africa Command area of responsibility operated by the Minnesota Air National Guard 133rd Airlift Wing at Camp Lemonnier, Djibouti, March 19, 2026. The C-130H3 is being retired by the unit in the fall, marking the final deployment of the 133rd Airlift Wing with these legacy aircraft.
